title: Oraclous Ingestion Recipe
$comment: Formalizes knowledge-graph-builder/docs/recipe-spec.md (TASK-220, STORY-034, ADR-022). A recipe is data, not code.

extractions.$comment: Hybrid free-text-on-a-field (Slice 2, #269): run the LLM entity extractor over a PROSE field within each structured record AFTER the deterministic projection, so records gain entities mined from their text and interconnect by shared entities. Each rule names a `from` field, an inline ontology (entity/relationship types, the Slice-B Ontology shape compiled to a hard GraphSchema), and a `link` edge MERGEd from the record's primary node to each extracted entity. Fail-soft: extractor unavailable (KGS_EXTRACTOR=null) → the pass is skipped, the deterministic projection is unaffected.

similarities.$comment: Content-similarity edges (Slice 3, #269): AFTER the deterministic projection, embed a designated `from` field per record and run a cosine kNN over those embeddings, MERGE-ing an `edge_type` (default SIMILAR_TO) edge between records whose text is similar (carrying a `score` float). One edge per UNORDERED pair (canonical min->max direction so a pair is written once). Fail-soft: an embed() failure → the whole pass is skipped, the projection + prior passes are unaffected.

safe_identifier.$comment: Cypher-safe identifier; the (?!__) guard rejects the ADR-015 reserved __wrapped__ namespace.

transform.$comment: Name of a deterministic value transform (domain/recipes/transforms.py: host|lower|strip_www) applied to the read value BEFORE it is used. The transform name is validated against the registry at recipe-validation time (unknown → RecipeValidationError).
One transform applied to EACH read identity field value (after normalize).

mapping.$comment: A mapping rule is a discriminated union on project_to; exactly one branch matches.

rule_node.$comment: A node rule projects ONE node per matched unit. When `from_each` is present it instead FANS OUT a list-valued field into one node per element (identity = the element value); a scalar value is treated as a 1-element list, an empty/missing field projects 0 nodes (skip + warn). `edge_to_each` pairs a fan-out node with an edge from the record's primary node to each element node.

from_each.$comment: A field:/column: ref to a LIST field on the matched record. Each element becomes one node whose identity is the element value (passed through the rule's identity.normalize + identity.transform); MERGE collapses equal elements across records to one shared node. With from_each, identity.from is ignored — the element value is the identity.

edge_to_each.$comment: Only valid alongside `from_each`. Projects an edge (record's primary node)-[type]->(each element node).

from_field.$comment: Required for resolve_by:foreign_key — a field:/column: ref on the SOURCE record holding the FK value(s) (scalar OR list). Each value becomes the target node_rule's identity, hashed with the engine's deterministic id; the edge MERGEs to that id, MERGE-ing a stub target if absent (cross-job/cross-file).

extraction_entity_type.$comment: An inline Slice-B EntityType: a node label + optional description/property names. The label must be a Cypher-safe identifier (it becomes a written :Label).

extraction_relationship_type.$comment: An inline Slice-B RelType: a relationship label + optional source/target entity names (used to build the GraphSchema patterns). The label must be Cypher-safe (it becomes a written rel type).

extraction_ontology.$comment: The inline ontology for one extraction rule (the Slice-B Ontology shape): typed entity defs (required, ≥1) + optional relationship defs + optional free-text steering hints. Compiled to a hard GraphSchema (to_graph_schema) + a prompt prefix (to_prompt_prefix); allowed_labels is derived from entity_types[].name, so strict/coerce enforcement applies to the extracted entities.

resolution.$comment: Entity resolution / canonicalization (Slice 4, #269), RESOLVE-ON-WRITE: canonicalize each extracted entity DURING ingestion so surface variants (Eurail / eurail.com / Eurail B.V.) MERGE onto ONE node keyed by the canonical key, never created as separate nodes. The node accumulates the original surface forms in an `aliases` set; a conservative semantic pass (resolution_pass.py) then clusters the canonical names per label, folding near-duplicates (>= merge_threshold) and flagging an ambiguous band ([candidate_threshold, merge_threshold)) with a SAME_AS_CANDIDATE edge.

canonical.$comment: When true, derive each entity's identity_key with the `canonical` transform (casefold + bare-domain stem + legal-suffix strip) instead of the plain casefold/whitespace normalize — so Eurail B.V. / eurail.com / Eurail Group collapse to one node.

aliases.$comment: Optional explicit alias map {<canonical_key>: [<surface_variant>, ...]}. A surface form matching a listed variant (after casefold) uses that canonical key, applied BEFORE the `canonical` transform — so domain-specific synonyms the transform cannot derive (e.g. a brand rename) still collapse.

merge_threshold.$comment: Cosine >= this (HIGH/conservative) folds two canonical nodes into one in the semantic pass. Must be >= candidate_threshold.

candidate_threshold.$comment: Cosine in [candidate_threshold, merge_threshold) is the AMBIGUOUS band: NOT merged — a SAME_AS_CANDIDATE {score} edge is MERGEd between the two canonical nodes for review.

fuzzy_threshold.$comment: Enrichment toward the legacy 4-pass dedup (#309). When > 0, an embedding-free, BLOCKED fuzzy stage folds canonical-name pairs (per label) whose difflib SequenceMatcher ratio >= this (HIGH/conservative, e.g. 0.92) BEFORE the semantic pass — catching typo/near-duplicate keys the `canonical` transform cannot collapse, even when the embedder is off/failing. 0 (default) disables it; the prior semantic-only behaviour is unchanged.

extraction.$comment: One hybrid extraction rule: mine entities from a record's `from` prose field (per the inline `ontology`) and MERGE a `link` edge from the record's primary node to each extracted entity. An optional `resolution` block canonicalizes the entities on write (Slice 4). An optional `temporal` flag adds LLM temporal extraction (Slice 1, #311).

from.$comment: A field:/column: ref to the PROSE field on each record whose text the extractor runs over (e.g. `field:claim`). Empty/missing text on a record is skipped.

temporal.$comment: When true (#311), append the relationship-temporal extraction steering to the prompt so the LLM mines valid_from/valid_to/event_time/event_time_end onto the relationships it emits; those keys are normalised (year-only -> full ISO date, blanks dropped) and written on the inter-entity edges. Off by default — a rule without it behaves exactly as before.

link.$comment: The edge from the record's primary node (the node `from_node_rule` projected for that record) to each entity extracted from the record's `from` text. `type` must be Cypher-safe; `from_node_rule` must reference a node rule in `mappings`.

similarity.$comment: One content-similarity rule (Slice 3, #269): embed each record's `from` prose field, run cosine kNN over the embeddings, and MERGE an `edge_type` edge between the per-record `node_rule` nodes of similar records (one edge per unordered pair, canonical min->max id direction; a `score` float property is stamped). `node_rule` must reference a `project_to: node` rule in `mappings`.

from.$comment: A field:/column: ref to the field on each record whose text is embedded (e.g. `field:claim`). A record with empty/missing text is skipped (it gets no similarity edges).

node_rule.$comment: The primary node rule whose per-record node the SIMILAR_TO edges connect — must reference a `project_to: node` rule in `mappings`.

edge_type.$comment: The Cypher-safe relationship type MERGEd between similar records (default SIMILAR_TO).

top_k.$comment: Keep at most this many nearest neighbours per record (after the min_score filter).

min_score.$comment: Minimum cosine similarity (0<min_score<=1) for an edge to be written. The DEFAULT floor; `per_type_min_score` overrides it for a specific target node LABEL (#310).

per_type_min_score.$comment: Per-node-type minimum-cosine overrides (#310, legacy similarity_service per-type thresholds: 0.85 chunks / 0.92 entities). A map of {<node label>: <min_score>}; the threshold for THIS rule is `per_type_min_score[<the node_rule's label>]` when present, else `min_score`. Lets one recipe set a looser floor for long-form text nodes and a stricter floor for short-name entity nodes (which embed more noisily) without authoring separate rules with magic numbers — the label makes the intent self-documenting. Keys are node labels; the looked-up label is resolved from the rule's `node_rule` mapping at run time.
